explanation: FALSE POSITIVE. While the uint8 loop counter in isMet would theoretically wrap to 0 after 255 iterations if conditionsLength >= 256, this precondition is impossible to satisfy. The only function that populates the conditions array — createNFTsFromTokens — enforces a hard cap of 2 conditions via the DuplicateOperatorInConditions check: each condition's operator must be either the sender or the receiver, and neither party may appear more than once (lines 222-251 of NFT.sol). No other code path adds elements to the conditions array. Therefore, conditions.length is bounded at 2, making the uint8 counter entirely safe and the described infinite-loop DoS unreachable through any on-chain interaction.
